mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS falls, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 310.0, it signals entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and declines in the unemployment rate indicate robust residential investment and labor market strength, which jointly produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when HOUST growth falls below 1% monthly, or forward returns show no significant difference from baseline during co-occurring signals.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend levels signals strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when real interest rates exceed 4.0%, or the signal shows negative predictive accuracy in >50% of expansionary quarters.
